title: Calling in Sick
learningGoal: The learner will independently produce short, functionally accurate medical self-reporting (excuse emails/notes) utilizing the target noun-possession structure ('I have a...') and avoiding syntactic transfer ('My head is paining').
instructions: You woke up feeling very sick! You need to write a short message to your boss explaining that you cannot come to work today. You must describe your symptoms correctly using English medical rules.

contentItems.text: CRITICAL RULE: Do not translate 'मेरा सिर दर्द कर रहा है' literally. You will lose points if you write the word 'Paining'!

writingPrompt: Write a 3-sentence message to your boss. 1. Say that you are sick today. 2. Say that you have a headache and a fever. 3. Say that you need to see a doctor.
promptGoal: Test independent generative application of English symptom-reporting structures ('I have a...', 'see a doctor') in a professional communication context.
referenceHints: Sentence 2: I [verb for possession] a headache and a fever.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: I am sick today. I have a headache and a fever. I need to see a doctor.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: आई एम सिक टुडे। आई हैव अ हेडेक एंड अ फीवर। आई नीड टू सी अ डॉक्टर।
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: मैं आज बीमार हूँ। मुझे सिरदर्द और बुखार है। मुझे डॉक्टर को दिखाने की ज़रूरत है।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect score. The learner utilized the 'I have a' possession structure perfectly and used the idiomatic 'see a doctor'.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: I am sick today. My head is paining and fever is there. I need doctor.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: आई एम सिक टुडे। माई हेड इज़ पेनिंग एंड फीवर इज़ देयर। आई नीड डॉक्टर।
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: व्याकरण की गंभीर गलतियाँ (Is paining, Fever is there)।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Critical failure. They completely fell back on literal Hindi translation rules ('paining', 'is there') instead of English noun frameworks.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the user correctly use 'I have a headache' and avoid the word 'paining' completely?
Did the user correctly use 'have a fever' and avoid 'fever is there'?
Did the user use the exact phrase 'see a doctor'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: I am sick today. I have a headache and a fever. I need to see a doctor.
acceptableAlternates: I am very sick today. I have a bad headache and a fever. I need to see the doctor.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Absolute zero tolerance for 'is paining' or 'suffering from' (unless perfectly idiomatic). Expectation is pure SVO possessive (I have a + noun).

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You wrote a perfect, native-sounding sick note! You correctly used 'I have a...' for both symptoms and totally avoided the 'paining' mistake.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review your writing. Did you write 'My head is paining'? We learned in the lesson that this is incorrect! You must say 'I have a headache'. Did you write 'I need doctor'? You must say 'I need TO SEE A doctor'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good start! Double check Sentence 3. 'Doctor' is a noun, so it needs action words in front of it. You can't just say 'I need doctor'. You must say 'I need TO SEE a doctor'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Write a message to your boss about being sick with a headache and fever.

hintText: Use the verbs 'AM', 'HAVE', and 'NEED'. (I AM sick. I HAVE a... I NEED to...)
commonMistakes: Writing 'I have a fever and headache' (dropping the article 'a' before headache).
